message:
feat(cli): add acfs status one-line health summary (bd-29fl)

New scripts/lib/status.sh providing fast (<100ms) system health check.
Supports --json, --short (prompt), and --check-updates modes.
Exit codes: 0=healthy, 1=warnings, 2=errors.

Updated acfs.zshrc: split 'status' from continue|progress routing,
added status subcommand and help entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1pjj","title":"Deep exploration: GIIL (Get Image from Internet Link)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of GIIL (GitHub Image Inline Links) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify GIIL installation
[[ -d /dp/giil ]] && echo "PASS: giil repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: giil repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v giil &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: giil command available" || { echo "FAIL: giil not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check for image processing tools
command -v convert &>/dev/null && echo "INFO: ImageMagick available" || echo "INFO: ImageMagick not installed"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/giil-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/giil/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for supported cloud services (iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos)

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - URL parsing for cloud services
  - Full-resolution image extraction
  - Authentication handling (if any)
  - Output format options
  - Batch processing capabilities

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
giil --help 2>&1 | head -20
giil download --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test with a sample URL (dry run if available)
giil --dry-run "https://example.com/image" 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No dry-run"
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'giil OR image download OR icloud photos'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'giil image extract' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/giil/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/giil && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Supported cloud services (iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos)
- [ ] URL parsing mechanism
- [ ] Full-resolution extraction method
- [ ] Output format options
- [ ] Batch processing

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] mail - image attachment handling
- [ ] wa - web automation for extraction

### 2.3 Service Verification
```bash
# Check supported services in code
grep -r "icloud\|dropbox\|google" /dp/giil/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10

# Check URL patterns supported
grep -r "pattern\|regex\|url" /dp/giil/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update giil entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Full-resolution image extraction
- `description`: Cloud service image download
- `deepDescription`: How extraction works per service
- `features`: Verified supported services
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test giil entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const giil = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'giil');
console.log('Testing giil entry...');
console.assert(giil, 'giil entry exists');
console.assert(giil.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(giil.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Image Extraction (Safe Mode)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/giil-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== GIIL E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Help
echo "Test 1: Help..." | tee -a $LOG
giil --help 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Version/status
echo "Test 2: Version..." | tee -a $LOG
giil --version 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No version flag"

# Test 3: Supported services
echo "Test 3: Services..." | tee -a $LOG
giil services 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No services command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-1pjj --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification

-{"id":"bd-1yfv","title":"Help button for stuck users in web wizard","description":"## Overview
Add a contextual "I'm stuck" help button in the web wizard that provides step-specific troubleshooting guidance.

## Current Problem
- Users get stuck and don't know what to do
- Generic FAQs don't address specific step issues
- No easy way to get help without leaving the wizard
- Common mistakes aren't addressed proactively

## Proposed Solution
- Floating "Need help?" button on each step
- Opens modal with step-specific troubleshooting
- Common issues and solutions for that step
- Links to relevant documentation
- Option to copy debug info for support

## Help Content Structure
```typescript
interface StepHelp {
  commonIssues: {
    symptom: string;
    solution: string;
    link?: string;
  }[];
  tips: string[];
  debugInfo: () => string;
}
```

## Example: SSH Step Help
**Common Issues:**
- "Permission denied" → Check file permissions (chmod 600)
- "Connection refused" → Verify VPS is running, check port 22
- "Host key verification" → Add host to known_hosts

**Tips:**
- Test connection before proceeding
- Use key-based auth, not passwords

## Implementation Details
1. Create `HelpButton` component (floating, fixed position)
2. Create `HelpModal` with tabbed content
3. Define help content for each wizard step
4. Track help button usage for analytics

## Debug Info Export
```
# ACFS Debug Info
Step: ssh-setup
OS: macOS 14.2
Browser: Chrome 120
State: { provider: "hetzner", sshKeyPath: "~/.ssh/id_rsa" }
Errors: []
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test help button appears on all steps
- [ ] Test modal opens/closes correctly
- [ ] Test content matches current step
- [ ] Test debug info generation
- [ ] Test mobile layout (button position)

## Files to Create
- apps/web/components/wizard/HelpButton.tsx
- apps/web/components/wizard/HelpModal.tsx
- apps/web/lib/stepHelp.ts (help content per step)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:21.414394609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:09:14.811272774Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yfv","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:26.493434121Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-21kh","title":"Progress bar during tool installation","description":"## Overview
Add a visual progress bar during tool installation showing overall progress and current tool being installed.

## Current Problem
- Installation can take several minutes
- Users see scrolling text but no sense of overall progress
- No way to estimate time remaining
- Anxiety: "Is it stuck or still working?"

## Proposed UI
```
Installing ACFS tools...
[████████████░░░░░░░░] 12/26 tools (46%)
Current: Installing rust toolchain...
```

## Implementation Details
1. Count total tools to install upfront
2. Update progress after each tool completes
3. Show current tool name during install
4. Use ANSI escape codes for in-place updates
5. Respect NO_COLOR environment variable

## Progress Calculation
- Parse manifest.yaml for tool count
- Subtract already-installed tools
- Increment counter after each install_tool() call

## Terminal Handling
```bash
# In-place update (same line)
printf '\r[%-20s] %d/%d tools (%d%%)' "$bar" "$done" "$total" "$pct"

# With NO_COLOR or non-TTY
echo "[$done/$total] Installing $tool..."
```

## Graceful Degradation
- Non-interactive: Simple line-by-line output
- NO_COLOR: Text-only progress
- Narrow terminal: Abbreviated format

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test progress updates correctly
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR fallback
- [ ] Test non-TTY output (piped)
- [ ] Test narrow terminal handling
- [ ] Visual test: progress matches actual completion

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh (add progress tracking)
- scripts/install.sh (initialize progress bar)
- New: scripts/lib/progress.sh (progress bar utilities)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:45.195454182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:07:22.822272754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21kh","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:54.194943376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Perform deep exploration of SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

```bash
# Check if slb daemon/server is available

```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/slb-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)

cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for two-person rule docs, coordination protocol

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Two-person rule implementation
  - Confirmation mechanism (how both agents confirm)
  - Timeout handling for confirmations
  - Integration with destructive command protection
  - Coordination protocol (messaging, locks)

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
slb --help 2>&1 | head -20
slb confirm --help 2>&1 | head -10
slb status --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
slb status 2>&1 | head -5
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'slb OR simultaneous launch OR two-person rule'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'slb coordination' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/simultaneous_launch_button && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Two-person rule mechanism
- [ ] Confirmation protocol and timeout
- [ ] Command types requiring two-person approval
- [ ] Integration with dcg (destructive command guard)
- [ ] Recovery from failed confirmations

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] dcg - destructive command protection
- [ ] mail - confirmation messaging
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent coordination

### 2.3 Safety Verification
```bash
# Check what commands trigger two-person rule
cat /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/config/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | head -20
# Or check code for command patterns
grep -r "dangerous\|destructive" /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update slb entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Two-person rule for dangerous commands
- `description`: Coordination protocol
- `deepDescription`: How confirmation works
- `features`: Verified safety capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations (dcg, mail, ntm)

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test slb entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const slb = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'slb');
console.log('Testing slb entry...');
console.assert(slb, 'slb entry exists');
console.assert(slb.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(slb.connectsTo?.includes('dcg'), 'connects to dcg');
console.log('All assertions passed');
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Coordination Flow (Safe Mode)
```bash

LOG=/tmp/slb-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== SLB E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG

slb pending 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No pending command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

```bash
br update bd-22gc --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

-{"id":"bd-29fl","title":"acfs status: One-line health summary command","description":"## Overview
Add `acfs status` command that outputs a single-line health summary, perfect for shell prompts or quick checks.

## Current Problem
- `acfs doctor` is verbose and takes time to run
- No quick way to check "is everything OK?"
- Shell prompt integration requires parseable output

## Proposed Output Format
```bash
$ acfs status
ACFS OK: 26 tools, last update 2h ago

$ acfs status  # with issues
ACFS WARN: 2 outdated tools, doctor recommended
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Everything healthy
- 1: Warnings (outdated tools, minor issues)
- 2: Errors (missing tools, broken state)

## Implementation Details
1. New file: `scripts/commands/status.sh`
2. Quick checks only (no network calls by default):
   - State file exists and valid
   - Required tools present in PATH
   - Last update timestamp
3. Optional `--check-updates` for network-based checks

## Machine-Readable Mode
```bash
$ acfs status --json
{"status":"ok","tools":26,"last_update":"2026-01-25T21:00:00Z","warnings":[]}
```

## Shell Prompt Integration
```bash
# Add to .bashrc
acfs_status() { acfs status --short 2>/dev/null || echo "?"; }
PS1='$(acfs_status) \w $ '
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test exit codes for all states
- [ ] Test --json output is valid JSON
- [ ] Test runs in < 100ms (no network)
- [ ] Test with missing state file
- [ ] Test with outdated state

## Files to Create

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# shellcheck disable=SC1091
+# ============================================================
+# ACFS Status - One-line health summary
+# Quick check: runs in <100ms, no network calls by default
+#
+# Exit codes:
+#   0 - Healthy (all core tools present, state valid)
+#   1 - Warnings (some optional tools missing, outdated state)
+#   2 - Errors (broken state, missing critical tools)
+#
+# Usage:
+#   acfs status                # Human-readable one-liner
+#   acfs status --json         # Machine-readable JSON
+#   acfs status --short        # Minimal output for shell prompts
+#   acfs status --check-updates  # Include network-based update check
+# ============================================================
+
+# --- PATH setup (matches doctor.sh) ---
+_status_ensure_path() {
+    local dir
+    for dir in \
+        "$HOME/.local/bin" \
+        "$HOME/.bun/bin" \
+        "$HOME/.cargo/bin" \
+        "$HOME/go/bin" \
+        "$HOME/.atuin/bin"; do
+        [[ -d "$dir" ]] || continue
+        case ":$PATH:" in
+            *":$dir:"*) ;;
+            *) export PATH="$dir:$PATH" ;;
+        esac
+    done
+}
+_status_ensure_path
+
+# --- Defaults ---
+_STATUS_JSON=false
+_STATUS_SHORT=false
+_STATUS_CHECK_UPDATES=false
+_ACFS_HOME="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}"
+
+# --- Parse args ---
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --json)           _STATUS_JSON=true; shift ;;
+        --short)          _STATUS_SHORT=true; shift ;;
+        --check-updates)  _STATUS_CHECK_UPDATES=true; shift ;;
+        --help|-h)
+            echo "Usage: acfs status [--json] [--short] [--check-updates]"
+            echo ""
+            echo "Quick one-line health summary."
+            echo ""
+            echo "Options:"
+            echo "  --json            Machine-readable JSON output"
+            echo "  --short           Minimal output for shell prompt integration"
+            echo "  --check-updates   Include network-based update checks (slower)"
+            echo ""
+            echo "Exit codes:"
+            echo "  0  Healthy"
+            echo "  1  Warnings (outdated, minor issues)"
+            echo "  2  Errors (broken state, missing critical tools)"
+            echo ""
+            echo "Examples:"
+            echo "  acfs status                     # Quick health check"
+            echo "  acfs status --json              # JSON for scripts"
+            echo "  acfs status --short             # For shell prompts"
+            echo "  acfs status --check-updates     # Check for ACFS updates"
+            echo ""
+            echo "Shell prompt integration:"
+            echo "  PROMPT='\$(acfs status --short 2>/dev/null) \w \$ '"
+            exit 0
+            ;;
+        *) shift ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+# --- Collect checks ---
+_warnings=()
+_errors=()
+_tool_count=0
+
+# Core tools: missing any of these is a warning
+_CORE_TOOLS=(zsh git tmux bun cargo go rg claude)
+# Optional tools: counted but not warned about
+_OPTIONAL_TOOLS=(codex gemini gh uv fzf zoxide atuin bat lsd ntm bv br cass cm slb ubs dcg)
+
+# 1. ACFS_HOME check
+if [[ ! -d "$_ACFS_HOME" ]]; then
+    _errors+=("ACFS_HOME missing")
+fi
+
+# 2. State file check
+_state_file="$_ACFS_HOME/state.json"
+if [[ ! -f "$_state_file" ]]; then
+    _errors+=("state file missing")
+elif [[ ! -s "$_state_file" ]]; then
+    _errors+=("state file empty")
+fi
+
+# 3. Count tools in PATH
+for cmd in "${_CORE_TOOLS[@]}"; do
+    if command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
+        ((_tool_count++))
+    else
+        _warnings+=("missing: $cmd")
+    fi
+done
+
+for cmd in "${_OPTIONAL_TOOLS[@]}"; do
+    if command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
+        ((_tool_count++))
+    fi
+done
+
+# 4. Last update timestamp
+_last_update_ts=""
+_last_update_human=""
+if [[ -f "$_state_file" ]]; then
+    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+        _last_update_ts=$(jq -r '
+            .last_completed_phase_ts //
+            .updated_at //
+            empty
+        ' "$_state_file" 2>/dev/null) || true
+    fi
+    if [[ -z "$_last_update_ts" ]]; then
+        _last_update_ts=$(sed -n 's/.*"updated_at"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' \
+            "$_state_file" 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
+    fi
+fi
+
+if [[ -n "$_last_update_ts" ]]; then
+    _last_epoch=$(date -d "$_last_update_ts" +%s 2>/dev/null) || _last_epoch=0
+    _now_epoch=$(date +%s)
+    if [[ "$_last_epoch" -gt 0 ]]; then
+        _age_secs=$((_now_epoch - _last_epoch))
+        if [[ $_age_secs -lt 3600 ]]; then
+            _last_update_human="$((_age_secs / 60))m ago"
+        elif [[ $_age_secs -lt 86400 ]]; then
+            _last_update_human="$((_age_secs / 3600))h ago"
+        else
+            _last_update_human="$((_age_secs / 86400))d ago"
+        fi
+    fi
+fi
+
+# 5. Optional: network-based update check
+_update_available=""
+if [[ "$_STATUS_CHECK_UPDATES" == "true" ]]; then
+    if [[ -f "$_ACFS_HOME/VERSION" ]]; then
+        _local_version=$(cat "$_ACFS_HOME/VERSION" 2>/dev/null) || _local_version=""
+        _remote_version=$(timeout 5 curl -fsSL \
+            "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/main/VERSION" \
+            2>/dev/null) || _remote_version=""
+        if [[ -n "$_remote_version" ]] && [[ -n "$_local_version" ]] \
+           && [[ "$_remote_version" != "$_local_version" ]]; then
+            _update_available="${_local_version} -> ${_remote_version}"
+            _warnings+=("update available: $_update_available")
+        fi
+    fi
+fi
+
+# --- Determine overall status ---
+_exit_code=0
+_status_word="OK"
+
+if [[ ${#_errors[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+    _exit_code=2
+    _status_word="ERROR"
+elif [[ ${#_warnings[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+    _exit_code=1
+    _status_word="WARN"
+fi
+
+# --- JSON escape helper (no jq dependency) ---
+_json_escape() {
+    local s="$1"
+    s=${s//\\/\\\\}
+    s=${s//\"/\\\"}
+    s=${s//$'\n'/\\n}
+    s=${s//$'\r'/\\r}
+    s=${s//$'\t'/\\t}
+    printf '%s' "$s"
+}
+
+# --- Output ---
+if [[ "$_STATUS_JSON" == "true" ]]; then
+    # Build JSON arrays without requiring jq
+    _warn_items=""
+    for w in "${_warnings[@]+"${_warnings[@]}"}"; do
+        [[ -z "$w" ]] && continue
+        [[ -n "$_warn_items" ]] && _warn_items+=","
+        _warn_items+="\"$(_json_escape "$w")\""
+    done
+
+    _err_items=""
+    for e in "${_errors[@]+"${_errors[@]}"}"; do
+        [[ -z "$e" ]] && continue
+        [[ -n "$_err_items" ]] && _err_items+=","
+        _err_items+="\"$(_json_escape "$e")\""
+    done
+
+    _last_update_json="null"
+    if [[ -n "$_last_update_ts" ]]; then
+        _last_update_json="\"$(_json_escape "$_last_update_ts")\""
+    fi
+
+    _update_json=""
+    if [[ -n "$_update_available" ]]; then
+        _update_json=",\"update_available\":\"$(_json_escape "$_update_available")\""
+    fi
+
+    printf '{"status":"%s","tools":%d,"last_update":%s,"warnings":[%s],"errors":[%s]%s}\n' \
+        "${_status_word,,}" "$_tool_count" "$_last_update_json" \
+        "$_warn_items" "$_err_items" "$_update_json"
+
+elif [[ "$_STATUS_SHORT" == "true" ]]; then
+    # Minimal output for shell prompts
+    case $_exit_code in
+        0) echo "OK" ;;
+        1) echo "WARN" ;;
+        2) echo "ERR" ;;
+    esac
+
+else
+    # Human-readable one-liner
+    _msg="ACFS $_status_word: $_tool_count tools"
+    [[ -n "$_last_update_human" ]] && _msg="$_msg, last update $_last_update_human"
+
+    if [[ ${#_errors[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        _msg="$_msg, ${#_errors[@]} error(s)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ ${#_warnings[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        _missing_count=0
+        for w in "${_warnings[@]}"; do
+            [[ "$w" == missing:* ]] && ((_missing_count++))
+        done
+        [[ $_missing_count -gt 0 ]] && _msg="$_msg, $_missing_count missing tool(s)"
+        [[ -n "$_update_available" ]] && _msg="$_msg, update available"
+    fi
+
+    case $_exit_code in
+        0) printf '\033[0;32m%s\033[0m\n' "$_msg" ;;
+        1) printf '\033[0;33m%s\033[0m\n' "$_msg" ;;
+        2) printf '\033[0;31m%s\033[0m\n' "$_msg" ;;
+    esac
+fi
+
+exit $_exit_code
